violations.finding: New code block contains executable bash control flow (if/else) and inline Python file-mutation logic (json.load, json.dump, file read/write) implementing the Step 5 spec_path patch. This is implementation code, not an example or documentation.
violations.recommendation: Extract to a parameterized script (e.g. scripts/patch-context-spec-path.py) and invoke via venv activation: source venv/bin/activate && python scripts/patch-context-spec-path.py --context-json <path> --spec-path <path>

violations.finding: Bare python3 heredoc invocation without venv activation. The diff adds 'CONTEXT_JSON_PATH="$CONTEXT_JSON" NEW_SPEC_PATH="$orchestrator_resolved_spec_path" python3 - <<PYEOF' which is a direct python3 call. Although the Python code uses only stdlib (json, sys, os), Standard 3 requires ALL python3 invocations use source venv.
violations.recommendation: Prefix with venv activation: source venv/bin/activate && CONTEXT_JSON_PATH=... python3 - <<'PYEOF', or extract to a named script invoked via venv.

standards_passed: no-hardcoded-values (Standard 2 -- hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh uses CLAUDE_HOME and PYTHON_BIN env-var chains; new regex at line 678 is a security-invariant constant for exact-match validation, not an environment-varying value; test files contain no hardcoded paths requiring parameterization; commands/dev.md new block uses shell variables for all paths)
integer-step-numbering (Standard 4 -- no decimal or lettered step patterns found in any changed .md file)

meaningful-naming (Standard 5 -- all changed files use descriptive names; test _v2 suffix is part of AC UID versioning, not a generic enhancement word; Standard 5 forbidden patterns target dash-v2 filenames like optimize-v2.py, not underscore-v2 embedded in acceptance-criteria identifiers)

english-only (Standard 6 -- unconditional scan of agents/*.md and commands/*.md found Chinese mascot rank labels at agents/style-inspector.md:333, exempt per spec-20260518-225715 S5.1; Chinese at hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh:1235 is pre-existing and outside the diff hunk 659-967; no Chinese in any diff hunk of changed files)
merge-similar-scripts (Standard 7 -- no similar scripts among changed files; test files serve distinct AC IDs; hook and command are unique in their function)
concise-documentation (Standard 8 -- commands/dev.md new block is 35 lines of procedural instructions plus a 5-row table; no marketing language, no verbose explanations, no repetitive examples)

parameterized-scripts (Standard 9 -- hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh uses env-var chains for CLAUDE_HOME, PYTHON_BIN, DAEMON_RESTART_GRANT_DIR; test files use REPO_ROOT derivation via pathlib; no unparameterized variable values in changed code)
dangling-references (Standard 10 -- no new script references added in the diff; all existing references in commands/dev.md verified to point to existing files)

merge-conflicts (Standard 11 -- detect-merge-conflicts.sh found 17 files with conflict markers, but NONE are in the changed-files scope; no merge conflict markers in commands/dev.md, hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh, or any test file)
observations_not_blocking: hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh:1235 (english-only) -- pre-existing Chinese text 'BLOCKED: 必须使用正常的UI流程创建session...' is outside the diff hunk (diff spans lines 659-967, this is at line 1235); introduced_in_diff: false; not blocking per AC-2.6

hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh:678 (no-hardcoded-values) -- codex flagged /root/.claude/scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py as hardcoded in _BULK_CANONICAL_RE regex; rejected because this is a security-invariant constant in a security validation regex — parameterizing it would allow CLAUDE_HOME manipulation to bypass the guard; Standard 2 exempts constants that genuinely never change, and security pattern-match constants are in that category

Standard 11 -- detect-merge-conflicts.sh found 17 files with conflict markers across the repo (INDEX.md, hooks/.env.example, hooks/README.md, etc.) but all are pre-existing and outside the changed-files scope

diff_overlap_analysis.rule_applied: AC-2.6 (b) line-level: findings are provably NEW when (i) their line falls within the cycle diff's changed-line range AND (ii) the finding is absent from the pre-diff baseline.
diff_overlap_analysis.violation_1_overlap: commands/dev.md:453-488 -- entirely new lines added by this diff (git diff confirms all lines prefixed with +). No pre-diff baseline content at these lines. introduced_in_diff: true.
diff_overlap_analysis.violation_2_overlap: commands/dev.md:466 -- new line within the same diff hunk. No pre-diff baseline. introduced_in_diff: true.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex returned 2 PROPOSED_FIX findings and 1 OBSERVATION_ONLY. Finding 1: missed Standard 1 violation at commands/dev.md:453-488 (inline executable code). Finding 2: missed Standard 2/9 violation at hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh:678 (hardcoded /root/.claude path in regex). Finding 3: confirmed Standard 3 finding is correct.

findings.issue: Missed Standard 1 no-inline-code violation at commands/dev.md:453-488 — new executable bash+Python code block with file-mutation logic
findings.proposed_fix: Add violation: standard=no-inline-code, location=commands/dev.md:453-488, finding=New command-file code block contains executable bash control flow and inline Python file-mutation logic, recommendation=Extract to scripts/patch-dev-context-spec-path.py
findings.rationale: Standard 1 is clear: command files MUST NOT contain inline bash/python code. The new block contains if/else control flow and Python file I/O. Even though the file has 10 pre-existing similar blocks, 'established pattern' is not a Standard 1 exemption. Applied as violation.

findings.issue: Missed Standard 2/9 violation at hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh:678 — hardcoded /root/.claude/scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py in security regex
findings.proposed_fix: Build escaped canonical writer path from ${CLAUDE_HOME}/scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py instead of hardcoding /root/.claude/
findings.rationale: This path is inside a security validation regex (_BULK_CANONICAL_RE) that performs exact-match assertions against bash commands. Security pattern-match constants MUST be literal — parameterizing them via CLAUDE_HOME would allow an attacker to manipulate the environment variable to bypass the security check. Standard 2 explicitly exempts 'Constants that genuinely never change'. The hook file already contains 20+ similar hardcoded security-pattern paths (session_dirs.txt, happy-session-recovery, happy-restart at lines 971-990) that serve the same purpose. Additionally, the pre-existing entry-gate regex at the previous version of line 662 also used literal paths. This is a security-invariant constant, not an environment-varying value.

findings.issue: Standard 3 finding at commands/dev.md:466 is not a false positive — confirmed as valid
findings.rationale: Codex confirmed the drafted Standard 3 finding. No change needed.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Applied codex finding #1 (Standard 1 violation added to violations[]). Rejected codex finding #2 (security-invariant constant in regex, not an environment-varying value). Logged codex finding #3 (confirmation of existing Standard 3 finding). Final violations: 2 (Standard 1 + Standard 3, both introduced_in_diff: true).
